summary.overall_regression: Critical regression: health score 65 → 20 (+45 pts). 3 critical incident(s).
summary.congestion_message: Congestion indicator — DB time/AAS increased +483% but transactions/sec decreased -40%. More resources consumed but fewer business transactions completed.
summary.causal_chain_text: 'enq: HW - contention' (86.4% DB time) → 'enq: TX - contention' (1.1% DB time)
summary.headline: DB Time rose 1102% driven by: bottleneck shift CPU→Mixed + 6 new application SQL(s) (top: anvdfxu0vqu6j, 444s) + new wait 'enq: HW - contention' (86.4% DB Time).
headline_evidence: 1 SQL(s) regressed without plan change (+1077343s cumulative)

comparisons.root_cause_hint: High-water mark extension lock. A segment is being extended by concurrent inserts — only one session holds the HW enqueue at a time, all others queue.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: High-water mark extension lock. A segment is being extended by concurrent inserts — only one session holds the HW enqueue at a time, all others queue.
pathology_investigate: which table/index is rapidly growing (DBA_SEGMENTS + AWR segment stats)

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Buffer being read or modified by another session — hot block contention.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Buffer being read or modified by another session — hot block contention.
pathology_investigate: top segments by buffer busy waits

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Time on CPU — actual execution work, not a wait. High CPU can indicate inefficient SQL or increased workload.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Time on CPU — actual execution work, not a wait. High CPU can indicate inefficient SQL or increased workload.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Single-block read — index lookup or rowid fetch. High count = many index scans. High avg latency = storage problem.
pathology_investigate: missing indexes causing unselective index scans

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Generic TX enqueue contention — encompasses row lock and index split paths.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Generic TX enqueue contention — encompasses row lock and index split paths.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Foreground session waiting for LGWR to flush redo to disk on COMMIT. >20ms avg = slow storage. High count = too many commits.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Redo allocation latch contention — high commit rate or large redo generation causing sessions to queue for redo allocation.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Redo allocation latch contention — high commit rate or large redo generation causing sessions to queue for redo allocation.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Parallel query or serial large table scan bypassing buffer cache. Common with parallel_degree_policy=AUTO.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Parallel query or serial large table scan bypassing buffer cache. Common with parallel_degree_policy=AUTO.
pathology_investigate: large table scans in top SQL

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Index block split contention from concurrent inserts into the same index leaf block range.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Index block split contention from concurrent inserts into the same index leaf block range.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Writing sort/hash results to TEMP. Same root cause as direct path read temp.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Writing sort/hash results to TEMP. Same root cause as direct path read temp.

comparisons.event_name: cursor: pin S wait on X
comparisons.root_cause_hint: Session waiting to read a cursor that another session is modifying (hard parsing). Signature of hard parse storm.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Session waiting to read a cursor that another session is modifying (hard parsing). Signature of hard parse storm.
pathology_investigate: hard parse rate in load profile

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Reading sort/hash results from TEMP tablespace — PGA insufficient, operation spilled to disk.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Reading sort/hash results from TEMP tablespace — PGA insufficient, operation spilled to disk.

comparisons.root_cause_hint: Redo copy latch — sessions waiting to copy redo into the log buffer. Indicates redo generation exceeds copy latch capacity.
comparisons.pathology_meaning: Redo copy latch — sessions waiting to copy redo into the log buffer. Indicates redo generation exceeds copy latch capacity.

recommendations.finding: Ratio inversion: transactions/sec changed -40% (349.80→209.40/s) while DB time/sec rose +483% (51.9→303.1s/s). Sessions are consuming more DB time but completing less work — classic wait-loop congestion.
recommendations.action: Sessions consuming more DB time but completing fewer transactions — wait-loop congestion. Identify dominant wait event and trace to root cause.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: 5 SQL statement(s) running significantly longer than baseline. Worst: SQL ahrw3nkrzt4ar — 27776s → 1105119s (+1831%). Likely plan regression, stale stats, or bind variable skew.
recommendations.action: Use Real-Time SQL Monitoring for execution-level visibility. Compare plans: SELECT * FROM TABLE(DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_AWR('<sql_id>')); Gather fresh statistics or use SQL Plan Baseline to lock good plan.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

recommendations.finding: 'enq: HW - contention' is a 6.7σ statistical outlier: 86.4% of DB time (baseline mean: 7.4%). This event is anomalously dominant in the bad period.
recommendations.action: High-water mark extension lock. A segment is being extended by concurrent inserts — only one session holds the HW enqueue at a time, all others queue.
recommendations.impact: Direct impact on DB availability or response time

incident_indicators.description: 5 SQL statement(s) running significantly longer than baseline. Worst: SQL ahrw3nkrzt4ar — 27776s → 1105119s (+1831%). Likely plan regression, stale stats, or bind variable skew.
incident_indicators.remediation: Use Real-Time SQL Monitoring for execution-level visibility. Compare plans: SELECT * FROM TABLE(DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_AWR('<sql_id>')); Gather fresh statistics or use SQL Plan Baseline to lock good plan.
incident_indicators.diagnostic_sql: SELECT sql_id, plan_hash_value, executions, elapsed_time/1e6 AS elapsed_s, cpu_time/1e6 AS cpu_s FROM dba_hist_sqlstat WHERE sql_id = 'ahrw3nkrzt4ar' ORDER BY snap_id DESC;

incident_indicators.description: 'enq: HW - contention' is a 6.7σ statistical outlier: 86.4% of DB time (baseline mean: 7.4%). This event is anomalously dominant in the bad period.
incident_indicators.remediation: High-water mark extension lock. A segment is being extended by concurrent inserts — only one session holds the HW enqueue at a time, all others queue.

all_metrics.label: DB Time/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Total database work per second. Core workload intensity metric.

all_metrics.label: DB CPU/sec
all_metrics.explanation: CPU consumed by database per second. Increasing means more compute demand.

all_metrics.label: Logical Reads/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Buffer gets per second. High values indicate intensive SQL scanning.

all_metrics.label: Physical Reads/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Disk read blocks per second. High value with low Read IO Requests/s = large multi-block full-table scans (direct-path, invisible to Segments section). High value with proportionally high Read IO Requests/s = many single-block index reads. Compare both metrics together.

all_metrics.label: Physical Writes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Disk writes per second. Increase may indicate more DML or redo/undo activity.

all_metrics.label: Redo Size/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Bytes of redo generated per second. Higher means more DML workload.

all_metrics.label: Hard Parses/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Hard parses per second. >100/s indicates literal SQL or cursor invalidation.

all_metrics.label: Executes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: SQL executions per second. Application throughput indicator.

all_metrics.label: Transactions/sec
all_metrics.explanation: User commits+rollbacks per second. Primary business throughput KPI.

all_metrics.label: Logons/sec
all_metrics.explanation: New session creations per second. Spike signals connection pool churn.

all_metrics.label: User Calls/sec
all_metrics.explanation: User-originated calls per second. Overall application request rate.

all_metrics.label: Block Changes/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Buffer blocks modified per second. Correlates with redo and DML volume.

all_metrics.label: Buffer Cache Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: Data found in RAM vs disk. <95% = excessive physical I/O.

all_metrics.label: Library Cache Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: SQL look-ups that found the cursor in memory. Low = shared pool too small.

all_metrics.label: Soft Parse %
all_metrics.explanation: SQL executions that reused a cached plan. <95% = hard parse storm.

all_metrics.label: Execute to Parse %
all_metrics.explanation: % executes reusing open cursor. Low = cursor caching not enabled.

all_metrics.label: Latch Hit %
all_metrics.explanation: Internal Oracle lock efficiency. <99% = contention in shared memory.

all_metrics.label: DB Time (min)
all_metrics.explanation: Total database active time. Core load indicator.

all_metrics.label: Avg Active Sessions
all_metrics.explanation: Sessions actively working. >CPU count = saturation.

all_metrics.label: Transactions/sec
all_metrics.explanation: Business throughput KPI (commits+rollbacks per second).

all_metrics.label: Elapsed Window
all_metrics.explanation: Observation window length. Large difference = unequal comparison.

all_metrics.label: enq: HW - contention
all_metrics.explanation: High-water mark extension lock. A segment is being extended by concurrent inserts — only one session holds the HW enqueue at a time, all others queue.

all_metrics.label: enq: TX - contention
all_metrics.explanation: Generic TX enqueue contention — encompasses row lock and index split paths.

all_metrics.label: enq: TX - index contention
all_metrics.explanation: Index block split contention from concurrent inserts into the same index leaf block range.

recommendations.finding: Log file sync average wait is 12.0ms, exceeding the 10ms threshold.  Commit latency is elevated.
recommendations.action: Move redo logs to faster storage or reduce commit frequency
recommendations.oracle_fix:
-- Move redo logs to SSD/flash
-- Consider COMMIT WRITE BATCH NOWAIT for non-critical transactions
recommendations.impact: Reduces commit latency and session queuing

recommendations.finding: CPU saturation detected: AAS=303.1 exceeds CPU count 52 x 1.5.
recommendations.action: Identify top CPU SQL, reduce parallelism, or scale CPU
recommendations.oracle_fix:
-- Identify top CPU consumers:
SELECT sql_id, cpu_time/1e6 cpu_s FROM v$sqlstats ORDER BY cpu_time DESC FETCH FIRST 10 ROWS ONLY;
recommendations.impact: Reduces response time and prevents runaway queueing

recommendations.finding: SQL_ID ahrw3nkrzt4ar regressed 3879% in elapsed time (27775.9s -> 1105119.3s).
recommendations.action: Analyze execution plan, check for plan flip.  Compare plans with DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_AWR.
recommendations.impact: Directly reduces DB time contribution of regressed SQL

insights.title: Workload Change: DB Time increased 1102%
evidence: Good: DB Time 1643.0 min, Elapsed 31.6 min, AAS 51.9
Bad: DB Time 19751.8 min, Elapsed 65.2 min, AAS 303.1
insights.root_cause: Significant workload increase — more SQL activity, higher concurrency, or data volume growth.
insights.action: Review the SQL and wait event analysis below to identify root cause.

insights.title: SQL Plan Regressions (1 SQLs)
insights.summary: 1 SQL statements got slower per execution while execution count stayed stable. This is a true SQL regression — likely caused by plan flips, stale statistics, or index changes.
evidence: ahrw3nkrzt4ar: per-exec 0.192s → 7.644s (+3883%), execs 144,736 → 144,575 (stable) — insert /* PlanArrivalPublisherHint */ into PLANARR
insights.root_cause: Execution plan changed due to: stale statistics (run DBMS_STATS), adaptive cursor sharing, index drop/rebuild, or parameter change.
insights.action: Compare execution plans: SELECT * FROM TABLE(DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_AWR(sql_id)). Lock good plans: EXEC DBMS_SPM.LOAD_PLANS_FROM_AWR. Gather fresh stats: EXEC DBMS_STATS.GATHER_TABLE_STATS.

insights.title: 6 New SQL Statements in Bad Period
insights.summary: 6 SQL IDs appeared only in the bad period, consuming 1382s total. These are either new application code, ad-hoc queries, or batch jobs that weren't running before.
evidence: anvdfxu0vqu6j: 444.0s, 0 execs — MERGE INTO U_DFUEXTFCST_AUDIT A USING (SELECT /*+
at1ygf4rd7cvj: 186.6s, 65636 execs — select file#, block#, blocks from seg$ where type#
aq0t5ju2h9tjh: 12.3s, 135420 execs — SELECT COUNT(*) FROM SRE_JOB, SRE_NODE_POOL_CONFIG
insights.root_cause: New code deployment, ad-hoc analysis queries, or different batch schedule.
insights.action: Identify source: SELECT MODULE, ACTION FROM V$SQLAREA WHERE SQL_ID IN (...). Check if these need indexes or plan optimization. Verify if these should have been running in this window.

insights.title: 3 New Wait Event Bottlenecks
insights.summary: These wait events appeared only in the bad period — they're entirely new contention points.
evidence: enq: TX - index contention: 4512.2s (0.4% DB time)
insights.root_cause: New contention from new SQL, changed concurrency, or infrastructure issues.
insights.action: Match each new wait event to the SQL or session causing it via ASH data.

insights.title: Incident: Ratio Inversion
insights.summary: Ratio inversion: transactions/sec changed -40% (349.80→209.40/s) while DB time/sec rose +483% (51.9→303.1s/s). Sessions are consuming more DB time but completing less work — classic wait-loop congestion.
insights.root_cause: Ratio inversion: transactions/sec changed -40% (349.80→209.40/s) while DB time/sec rose +483% (51.9→303.1s/s). Sessions are consuming more DB time but completing less work — classic wait-loop congestion.
insights.action: Sessions consuming more DB time but completing fewer transactions — wait-loop congestion. Identify dominant wait event and trace to root cause.

insights.title: Incident: Long Running Sql Regression
insights.summary: 5 SQL statement(s) running significantly longer than baseline. Worst: SQL ahrw3nkrzt4ar — 27776s → 1105119s (+1831%). Likely plan regression, stale stats, or bind variable skew.
insights.root_cause: 5 SQL statement(s) running significantly longer than baseline. Worst: SQL ahrw3nkrzt4ar — 27776s → 1105119s (+1831%). Likely plan regression, stale stats, or bind variable skew.
insights.action: Use Real-Time SQL Monitoring for execution-level visibility. Compare plans: SELECT * FROM TABLE(DBMS_XPLAN.DISPLAY_AWR('<sql_id>')); Gather fresh statistics or use SQL Plan Baseline to lock good plan.

insights.title: Incident: Zscore Wait Anomaly
insights.summary: 'enq: HW - contention' is a 6.7σ statistical outlier: 86.4% of DB time (baseline mean: 7.4%). This event is anomalously dominant in the bad period.
insights.root_cause: 'enq: HW - contention' is a 6.7σ statistical outlier: 86.4% of DB time (baseline mean: 7.4%). This event is anomalously dominant in the bad period.
insights.action: High-water mark extension lock. A segment is being extended by concurrent inserts — only one session holds the HW enqueue at a time, all others queue.

insights.title: ⚠ Baseline Already Stressed — Not a Clean Healthy Reference
insights.summary: The baseline period itself was near-saturated (AAS=51.9 vs 52 CPUs = 100% utilization). The problem period degradation is ON TOP OF an already overloaded system. Pre-existing ADDM findings: Undersized SGA, Buffer Busy - Hot Objects. Performance deltas may understate the true severity.
evidence: Baseline latch hit: 98.8% (degraded — not newly introduced in problem period)
ADDM baseline finding: Buffer Busy - Hot Objects
insights.root_cause: The 'good' period was not a clean healthy baseline — it was already at or near capacity. Issues visible in the problem period may have pre-existing roots.
insights.action: Compare against a genuinely idle/healthy period if available. Look at ADDM findings for the baseline period — any findings indicate pre-existing issues. Do not attribute all problem-period issues solely to new workload.

insights.title: Wait Profile Shifted — Configuration increased
insights.summary: The extra DB time is primarily going to Configuration (1% → 86%).
insights.root_cause: Wait class 'Configuration' changed by +85.6 percentage points.
insights.action: Cross-reference this with the SQL analysis below to find the specific queries driving this shift.

insights.title: Data Volume Increase Detected (1 SQLs)
insights.summary: 1 SQL statements show increased execution counts without per-execution time changes. This indicates data volume growth, not SQL regression. The queries themselves are fine — there's simply more data to process.
evidence: 87r4zf1m45pkf: execs 466,300 → 1,543,293 (+231%), per-exec 0.000s → 0.000s (stable) — SELECT SSO_ID, SESSION_ID, CALLBACK_URL, USER_NAME
insights.root_cause: Data volume increased between the two periods. More rows to process means more executions of the same queries. Check: batch input data size, table row counts, partition growth.
insights.action: Verify data volume change with: SELECT COUNT(*) and partition stats. If volume growth is expected, consider partitioning, parallel DML, or batch scheduling changes. These are NOT SQL tuning candidates — the per-execution time is stable.

analysis_notes: RULE: ADDM recommendations are guides, not gospel. Validate each recommendation against multiple AWR periods before making system changes. Do NOT increase SGA/PGA based on a single AWR report alone — gather at least 3–5 representative periods.
RULE: AWR averages hide spikes. A 60-minute snapshot can mask a 5-minute crisis. If symptoms are intermittent, split into 15-minute reports or use ASH.
RULE: Buffer Cache Hit Ratio is not a primary health signal. A 99% hit ratio can coexist with a burning system. Investigate wait events and per-execution SQL metrics instead.

investigation_trail.finding: AAS = 51.9 vs 52 CPUs — moderate load, within capacity, OS CPU busy: 44%
investigation_trail.conclusion: Database is busy but within CPU capacity. Proceed to bottleneck analysis.

investigation_trail.finding: Snapshot window is 32 minutes — averages hide spikes.
investigation_trail.conclusion: AWR averages can mask a 5-minute crisis inside a 60-minute window. If symptoms are intermittent, split into 15-minute reports (awrrpt.sql) or use ASH (DBA_HIST_ACTIVE_SESS_HISTORY) to isolate the exact problem period.

investigation_trail.finding: #1 non-idle wait: 'DB CPU' at 42.8% DB time — this is the symptom.
investigation_trail.conclusion: Primary bottleneck category: CPU. Next: find Top SQL by Elapsed that links to this wait, then check Segment Statistics to confirm which object is under pressure.

investigation_trail.finding: Top SQL ahrw3nkrzt4ar at 28% DB time — SQL ahrw3nkrzt4ar: 144,736 execs at 0.192s/exec — high volume but low per-exec cost. Total elapsed is high due to frequency, not because the query is slow. Reduce call frequency or cache results.
investigation_trail.conclusion: RULE: Always evaluate per-execution cost, not just totals. High total elapsed from many cheap calls = volume problem. High per-exec cost from few calls = broken query. Check execution plans for full-table scans, missing indexes, and bind variable usage.

investigation_trail.finding: Buffer Hit 99.8%, Soft Parse 99.9%, Library Hit 100.0%, Exec to Parse 95.2%, Latch Hit 98.8%
investigation_trail.conclusion: All efficiency metrics within thresholds.

findings.title: Configuration/Resource Sizing Bottleneck
findings.detail: Configuration wait class consuming 86.4% of DB time. Events: enq: HW - contention. This requires administrative/DDL action, NOT SQL tuning.

findings.title: Segment Extension Bottleneck (enq: HW): 86.4% DB Time
findings.detail: High Water Mark enqueue contention: 86.4% DB time, avg 7831.8ms, 130,770 waits. Multiple sessions trying to extend the SAME segment beyond its HWM. Only ONE session holds HW enqueue at a time — all others queue. Avg latency >1000ms = SEVERE segment extension bottleneck. Top INSERT SQL: ahrw3nkrzt4ar (144,575 execs) — its target table is likely the hot segment. ADDM confirms 'High Watermark Waits' — root cause CONFIRMED with highest confidence.

findings.title: Single SQL Dominates: 93% of DB Time
findings.detail: SQL ahrw3nkrzt4ar consuming 93% of total DB time. Executions: 144,575, Per-exec: 7.64s, Buffer Gets/Exec: 23,727, Disk Reads/Exec: 0. This SQL IS the bottleneck.

sql_stats.sql_text: DELETE /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANARRIV pa, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = pa.Item AND s.Loc = pa.Dest AND sp.Item = s.Item AND sp.Loc = s.loc AND ( ( pa.SchedArrivDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_03", :"SYS_B_04") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_05", :"SYS_B_06") THEN s.OHPost END)+ sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_07", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_08", :"SYS_B_09") then case when l.ohpost > TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_10", :"SYS_B_11") then l.ohpost else s.ohpost end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.DrpTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_12", sp.DrpTimeFenceDate)) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_13") OR ( pa.SchedArrivDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_14", :"SYS_B_15") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_16", :"SYS_B_17") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_18", :"SYS_B_19") THEN s.OHPost END) + sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_20", GREATEST((CASE WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_21", :"SYS_B_22") THEN s.OHPost WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN l.OHPost ELSE s.OHPost END)) + sp.PlanLeadTime/:"SYS_B_25")) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_26" ) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_27")
sql_stats.sql_text_full: DELETE /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANARRIV pa, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = pa.Item AND s.Loc = pa.Dest AND sp.Item = s.Item AND sp.Loc = s.loc AND ( ( pa.SchedArrivDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_03", :"SYS_B_04") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_05", :"SYS_B_06") THEN s.OHPost END)+ sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_07", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_08", :"SYS_B_09") then case when l.ohpost > TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_10", :"SYS_B_11") then l.ohpost else s.ohpost end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.DrpTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_12", sp.DrpTimeFenceDate)) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_13") OR ( pa.SchedArrivDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_14", :"SYS_B_15") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_16", :"SYS_B_17") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_18", :"SYS_B_19") THEN s.OHPost END) + sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_20", GREATEST((CASE WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_21", :"SYS_B_22") THEN s.OHPost WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN l.OHPost ELSE s.OHPost END)) + sp.PlanLeadTime/:"SYS_B_25")) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_26" ) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_27")

sql_stats.sql_text: SELECT /*+ PARALLEL(32) */ /* CalculatePlanSelectHint */ SCPOMGR.ITEM.ITEM, SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC, SCPOMGR.ITEM.PLANLEVEL, SCPOMGR.SKU.PLANLEVEL, DECODE(SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.EXTERNALSKUSW, :"SYS_B_00", :"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02", :"SYS_B_03") , SCPOMGR.SKU.NETCHGSW as NETCHGSWORG , -:"SYS_B_04" FROM SCPOMGR.SKU , SCPOMGR.ITEM , SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM, SCPOMGR.LOC RELX_LOC where SCPOMGR.SKU.ITEM = SCPOMGR.ITEM.ITEM and SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.ITEM = SCPOMGR.SKU.ITEM and SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.LOC = SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC and (((RELX_LOC.LOC_TYPE = :"SYS_B_05" OR RELX_LOC.LOC_TYPE = :"SYS_B_06") AND (RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_07" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_08" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_09" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_10" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_11" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_12" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = : "SYS_B_13" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_14") AND (RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_15" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_16" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_17" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_18" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_19" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_20" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_21" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_22" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_23" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_24" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_25" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_26")) OR (RELX_LOC.LOC_TYPE = :"SYS_B_27" AND RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_28" AND (RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_29" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_30" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_31" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_32" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_33" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_34"))) and SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC=RELX_LOC.LOC ORDER B Y item.planlevel, item.item
sql_stats.sql_text_full: SELECT /*+ PARALLEL(32) */ /* CalculatePlanSelectHint */ SCPOMGR.ITEM.ITEM, SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC, SCPOMGR.ITEM.PLANLEVEL, SCPOMGR.SKU.PLANLEVEL, DECODE(SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.EXTERNALSKUSW, :"SYS_B_00", :"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02", :"SYS_B_03") , SCPOMGR.SKU.NETCHGSW as NETCHGSWORG , -:"SYS_B_04" FROM SCPOMGR.SKU , SCPOMGR.ITEM , SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM, SCPOMGR.LOC RELX_LOC where SCPOMGR.SKU.ITEM = SCPOMGR.ITEM.ITEM and SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.ITEM = SCPOMGR.SKU.ITEM and SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.LOC = SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC and (((RELX_LOC.LOC_TYPE = :"SYS_B_05" OR RELX_LOC.LOC_TYPE = :"SYS_B_06") AND (RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_07" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_08" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_09" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_10" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_11" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_12" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = : "SYS_B_13" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_14") AND (RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_15" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_16" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_17" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_18" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_19" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_20" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_21" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_22" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_23" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_24" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_25" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_26")) OR (RELX_LOC.LOC_TYPE = :"SYS_B_27" AND RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_28" AND (RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_29" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_30" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_31" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_32" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_33" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_34"))) and SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC=RELX_LOC.LOC ORDER B Y item.planlevel, item.item

MA.MAX_SQL_EXEC_START) * 86400), LEAST(MA.SUM_ELAPSED_TIME/1000000, 1), CEIL(MA.MAX_QUEUING_TIME/1000000))), DECODE(MA.SUM_ELAPSED_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('elapsed_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_ELAPSED_TIME)), DECODE(MA.MAX_QUEUING_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('queuing_time' AS "name"), MA.MAX_QUEUING_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_CPU_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cpu_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_CPU_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_USER_IO_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('user_io_wait_time' AS "name "), MA.SUM_USER_IO_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_APPLICATION_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('application_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_APPLICATION_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_CONCURRENCY_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('concurrency_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_CONCURRENCY_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_CLUSTER_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cluster_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_CLUSTER_WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_PLSQL_EXEC_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('plsql_exec_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_PLSQL_EXEC_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_JAVA_EXEC_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('java_exec_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_JAVA_EXEC_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_OTHER_WAIT_TIME, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('other_wait_time' AS "name"), MA.SUM_OTHER_ WAIT_TIME)), DECODE(MA.SUM_FETCHES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('user_fetch_count' AS "name"), MA.SUM_FETCHES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_BUFFER_GETS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('buffer_gets' AS "name"), MA.SUM_BUFFER_GETS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_READ_REQS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('read_reqs' AS "name"), MA.SUM_READ_REQS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_READ_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('read_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_READ_BYTES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_WRITE_REQS, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('write_reqs' AS "name"), MA.SUM_WRITE_REQS)), DECODE(MA.SUM_WRITE_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('write_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_WRITE_BYTES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('unc_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYT ES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('elig_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES)), DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES, 0, NULL, XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('ret_bytes' AS "name"), MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES)), CASE WHEN MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES IS NULL OR MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES = MA.SUM_IO_BYTES OR MA.SUM_IO_BYTES = 0 OR NVL(MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 0) = 0 THEN NULL ELSE XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cell_offload_efficiency' AS "name"), ROUND(MA.SUM_IO_BYTES / DECODE(MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES, 0, 1, MA.SUM_IO_INTER_BYTES), 2)) END, CASE WHEN NVL(MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 0) = 0 OR MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES = MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES THEN NULL ELSE XMLELEMENT( "stat", XMLATTRIBUTES('cell_offload_efficiency2' AS "name"), GREATEST(0, ROUND(100 * (MA.SUM_IO_UNC_BYTES - MA.SUM_IO_RET_BYTES) / MA.SUM_IO_ELIG_BYTES, 2))) END) ) END) FROM MONITOR_AGG MA) ELSE NULL END) FROM ( SELECT CASE WHEN V1.XPLAN_XML IS NULL OR V1.XPLAN_XML.EXISTSNODE('/error') > 0 THEN NULL ELSE V1.XPLAN_XML END XPLAN_XML FROM (SELECT CASE WHEN :B36 = 1 THEN DBMS_XPLAN.BUILD_PLAN_XML( 3dbzmtf9ahvzt.fullText: merge /* KSXM:OPTIM_DML_INF */ into sys.mon_mods_all$ m using dual on (m.obj# = :objn and (:pobjn = 0 or m.obj# = :pobjn or m.obj# in (select obj# from tabpart$ where bo# = :pobjn and obj# = :objn union all select obj# from tabcompart$ where bo# = :pobjn and obj# = :objn union all select ts p.obj# from tabsubpart$ tsp, tabcompart$ tcp where tsp.pobj# = tcp.obj# and (tcp.bo# = :pobjn or tcp.obj# = :pobjn) and tsp.obj# = :objn))) when matched then update set inserts = decode(:reset, 1, 0, inserts) + :ins, updates = decode(:reset, 1, 0, updates) + :upd, deletes = decode(:reset, 1, 0, deletes) + :del, flags = decode(:reset, 1, 0, flags - bitand(flags, :flag)) + :flag, drop_segments = decode(:reset, 1, 0, drop_segments) + :dropseg, timestamp = :time when NOT matched then insert (obj#, inserts, updates, deletes, timestamp, flags, drop_segments) values (:objn, :ins, :upd, :del, :time, :flag, :dropseg) where :ins != 0 or :upd != 0 or :del != 0 or :flag != 0 or :dropseg !=0

6cmgg10unqfur.fullText: SELECT /* PlanShipTVQHint */ planarriv.item, planarriv.source, planarriv.schedshipdate, planarriv.expdate, planarriv.firmplansw, planarriv.nonignorableqty, planarriv.qty, planarriv.shrinkageqty, planarriv.substqty FROM PROCESSSKU, PLANARRIV WHERE ( PLANARRIV.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND PLANARRIV.SOURCE=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3 ) ) ORDER BY planarriv.item, planarriv.source, planarriv.schedshipdate ASC

6sbg5ynywhk0z.fullText: DELETE /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANORDER po, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = po.Item AND s.Loc = po.Loc AND sp.loc = s.loc AND sp.item = s.item AND ( ( po.SchedDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN TRUNC(l.FrzStart, :"SYS_B_03") WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_04", :"SYS_B_05") THEN TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_06") WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_07", :"SYS_B_08") THEN TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_09") END ) + sp.MfgFrzDur/:"SYS_B_10", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE(:"SYS_B_11", :"SYS_B_12") then case when l.OHPost > TO_DATE(:"SYS_B_13", :"SYS_B_14") then TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_15") else TRUNC( s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_16") end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.MpsTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_17", TRUNC(sp.MPSTIMEFENCEDATE, :"SYS_B_18"))) AND po.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_19") OR ( po.SchedDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_20", :"SYS_B_21") THEN TRUNC(l.FrzStart, :"SYS_B_22") WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_25") WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_26", :"SYS_B_27") THEN TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_28") END ) + sp.MfgFrzDur/:"SYS_B_29", GREATEST(TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_30"), TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_31")) + sp.MfgLeadTime/:"SYS_B_32") )) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_33" )

7fyqfjhzfpy4h.fullText: SELECT /* CustOrderNonFcstTVQHint */ custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate, custorder.qty, custorder.reservation, custorder.resexp, custorder.supersedesw FROM PROCESSSKU, CUSTORDER WHERE ((custorder.fcstsw = :"SYS_B_0" and custorder.status != :"SYS_B_1")) AND ( CUSTORDER.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND CUSTORDER.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3 ) ) ORDER BY custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate ASC

7hfj1a7q6vd9h.fullText: DELETE /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANARRIV pa, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = pa.Item AND s.Loc = pa.Dest AND sp.Item = s.Item AND sp.Loc = s.loc AND ( ( pa.SchedArrivDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_03", :"SYS_B_04") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_05", :"SYS_B_06") THEN s.OHPost END)+ sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_07", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_08", :"SYS_B_09") then case when l.ohpost > TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_10", :"SYS_B_11") then l.ohpost else s.ohpost end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.DrpTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_12", sp.DrpTimeFenceDate)) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_13") OR ( pa.SchedArrivDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_14", :"SYS_B_15") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_16", :"SYS_B_17") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_18", :"SYS_B_19") THEN s.OHPost END) + sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_20", GREATEST((CASE WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_21", :"SYS_B_22") THEN s.OHPost WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN l.OHPost ELSE s.OHPost END)) + sp.PlanLeadTime/:"SYS_B_25")) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_26" ) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_27")

7n9pz49qw4f39.fullText: select decode(upper(failover_method), NULL, 0 , 'BASIC', 1, 'PRECONNECT', 2 , 'PREPARSE', 4 , 0), decode(upper(failover_type), NULL, 1, 'NONE', 1, 'SESSION', 2, 'SELECT', 4, 'TRANSACTION', 8, 1), failover_retries, failover_delay, flags, nvl(replay_initiation_timeout, 300), decode(upper(session_state_consistency), 'STATIC', 16, 0), failover_restore from sys.service$ where name = :1

85d154rq6wjur.fullText: SELECT /* CustOrderFcstTVQHint */ custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate, custorder.fcsttype, custorder.qty, custorder.reservation, custorder.resexp, custorder.supersedesw FROM PROCESSSKU, CUSTORDER WHERE ((custorder.fcstsw = :"SYS_B_0" and custorder.status != :"SYS_B_1")) AND ( CUSTORDER.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND CUSTORDER.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3 ) ) ORDER BY custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate ASC

865qwpcdyggkk.fullText: select spare6 from user$ where user#=:1

87fzyp4nvktqr.fullText: UPDATE SRE_NODE SET LAST_HEARTBEAT = SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) WHERE NODE_ID = :B2 AND ((LAST_HEARTBEAT IS NULL) OR ((SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) - LAST_HEARTBEAT) > :B1 ) OR ((LAST_HEARTBEAT - SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP)) > :B1 ) )

87r4zf1m45pkf.fullText: SELECT SSO_ID, SESSION_ID, CALLBACK_URL, USER_NAME, PRINCIPAL_ID, DIRTY_FLAG FROM SSO_MAP where SSO_ID = :1

SCPOMGR.PBFSTATS.MODEL=SCPOMGR.DFU.MODEL and SCPOMGR.FCSTPERFSTATIC.LAG = :"SYS_B_0" and SCPOMGR.FCSTPERFSTATIC.FCSTDATE = to_date(:"SYS_B_1", :"SYS_B_2") and SCPOMGR.PBFSTATS.D MDUNIT=SCPOMGR.DMDUNIT.DMDUNIT and SCPOMGR.PBFSTATS.DMDGROUP=SCPOMGR.DMDGROUP.DMDGROUP and SCPOMGR.PBFSTATS.LOC=SCPOMGR.LOC.LOC AND (SCPOMGR.DFU.DMDUNIT, SCPOMGR.DFU.DMDGROUP, SCPOMGR.DFU.LOC, SCPOMGR.DFU.MODEL) in (SELECT A0, A1, A2, A3 FROM ( SELECT DISTINCT SCPOMGR.DFU.DMDUNIT as A0 , SCPOMGR.DFU.DMDGROUP as A1 , SCPOMGR.DFU.LOC as A2 , SCPOMGR.DFU.MODEL as A3 FROM SCPOMGR.DFU, SCPOMGR.LOC RELX_LOC, SCPOMGR.DFUVIEW RELX_DFUVIEW, SCPOMGR.DMDUNIT RELX_DMDUNIT where SCPOMGR.DFU.LOC = :"SYS_B_3" and (SCPOMGR.DFU.DMDUNIT = :"SYS_B_4" AND (SCPOMGR.DFU.LOC = :"SYS_B_5" OR SCPOMGR.DFU.LOC = :"SYS_B_6")) and SCPOMGR.DFU.LOC=RELX_LOC.LOC and SCPOMGR.DFU.DMDUNIT=RELX_DMDUNIT.DMDUNIT and SCPOMGR.DFU.DMDUNIT=RELX_DFUVIEW.DMDUNIT AND SCPOMGR.DFU.DMDGROUP=RELX_DFUVIEW.DMDGROUP AND SCPOMGR.DFU.LOC=RELX_DFUVIEW.LOC ORDER BY SCPOMGR.DFU.DMD UNIT, SCPOMGR.DFU.DMDGROUP, SCPOMGR.DFU.LOC, SCPOMGR.DFU.MODEL)) and (SCPOMGR.DFU.DMDUNIT = :"SYS_B_7" AND (SCPOMGR.DFU.LOC = :"SYS_B_8" OR SCPOMGR.DFU.LOC = :"SYS_B_9")) and SCPOMGR.DFU.LOC=SCPOMGR.LOC.LOC and SCPOMGR.DFU.DMDUNIT=SCPOMGR.DMDUNIT.DMDUNIT and SCPOMGR.DFU.DMDUNIT=RELX_DFUVIEW.DMDUNIT AND SCPOMGR.DFU.DMDGROUP=RELX_DFUVIEW.DMDGROUP AND SCPOMGR.DFU.LOC=RELX_DFUVIEW.LOC group by SCPOMGR.PBFSTATS.DMDUNIT, SCPOMGR.PBFSTATS.DMDGROUP, SCPOMGR.PBFSTATS.LOC, SCPOMGR.PBFSTATS.MODEL, SCPOMGR.FCSTPERFSTATIC.ABSPCTTOTERROR, SCPOMGR.PBFSTATS.MEAN , SCPOMGR.DMDUNIT.DESCR, SCPOMGR.DMDGROUP.DESCR, SCPOMGR.LOC.DESCR

SID, SAMPLE_TIME, TIME_INTERVAL, LENGTH_SEG, TM_LENGTH_SEG, END_SEG - TM_LENGTH_SEG TM_START_SEG, END_SEG - LENGTH_SEG START_SEG, END_SEG, TM_DELTA_CPU_TIME, TM_DELTA_DB_TIME, DELTA_READ_IO_REQUESTS, DELTA_WRITE_IO_REQUESTS, DELTA_READ_IO_BYTES, DELTA_WRITE_IO_BYTES, DELTA_INTERCONNECT_IO_BYTES, DELTA_READ_MEM_BYTES, PGA_ALLOCATED, TEMP_SPACE_ALLOCATED FROM (SELECT ROW_ID, SID, SAMPLE_TIME, EXTRACT(SECOND FROM REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT) + EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT)*60 + EXTRACT(HOUR FROM REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT)*3600 + EXTRACT(DAY FROM REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT)*3600*24 END_SEG, TIME_INTERVAL, DELTA_TIME/1000000 LENGTH_SEG, TM_DELTA_TIME/1000000 TM_LENGTH_SEG, TM_DELTA_CPU_TIME, TM_DELTA_DB_TIME, DELTA_READ_IO_REQUESTS, DELTA_WRITE_IO_REQUESTS, DELTA_READ_IO_BYTES, SAMPLE_DATE, SAMPLE_TIME, (SAMPLE_TIME - CAST(:B6 AS TIMESTAMP)) REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT, TRUNC((:B5 - :B6 ) * 3600 * 24) TIME_INTERVAL, :B6 AS BND_START_INTERVAL , :B5 AS BND_END_INTERVAL , DELTA_READ_MEM_BYTES, PGA_ALLOCATED, TEMP_SPACE_ALLOCATED FROM (SELECT ASH0.* FROM V$ACTIVE_SESSION_HISTORY ASH0 WHERE ((SESSION_ID = :B2 AND SESSION_SERIAL# = :B1 AND USERENV('INSTANCE') = :B3 ) OR (:B4 = 'yes' AND QC_INSTANCE_ID = :B3 AND QC_SESSION_ID = :B2 AND QC_SESSION_SERIAL# = :B1 ))) ) ASH1 WHERE SAMPLE_DATE >= BND_START_INTERVAL AND SAMPLE_DATE <= BND_END_INTERVAL + 120/3600/24 ) ) TM_GROUP_TYPE, TM_BUCKET_START, TM_BUCKET_END))) GVTF WHERE INST_ID >= :B9 AND INST_ID <= :B8 GROUP BY GROUP_TYPE, BUCKET_START , BUCKET_END, TM_GROUP_TYPE, TM_BUCKET_START, TM_BUCKET_END ) GROUP BY _sql_text_map.3dbzmtf9ahvzt: merge /* KSXM:OPTIM_DML_INF */ into sys.mon_mods_all$ m using dual on (m.obj# = :objn and (:pobjn = 0 or m.obj# = :pobjn or m.obj# in (select obj# from tabpart$ where bo# = :pobjn and obj# = :objn union all select obj# from tabcompart$ where bo# = :pobjn and obj# = :objn union all select ts p.obj# from tabsubpart$ tsp, tabcompart$ tcp where tsp.pobj# = tcp.obj# and (tcp.bo# = :pobjn or tcp.obj# = :pobjn) and tsp.obj# = :objn))) when matched then update set inserts = decode(:reset, 1, 0, inserts) + :ins, updates = decode(:reset, 1, 0, updates) + :upd, deletes = decode(:reset, 1, 0, deletes) + :del, flags = decode(:reset, 1, 0, flags - bitand(flags, :flag)) + :flag, drop_segments = decode(:reset, 1, 0, drop_segments) + :dropseg, timestamp = :time when NOT matched then insert (obj#, inserts, updates, deletes, timestamp, flags, drop_segments) values (:objn, :ins, :upd, :del, :time, :flag, :dropseg) where :ins != 0 or :upd != 0 or :del != 0 or :flag != 0 or :dropseg !=0

_sql_text_map.6sbg5ynywhk0z: DELETE /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANORDER po, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = po.Item AND s.Loc = po.Loc AND sp.loc = s.loc AND sp.item = s.item AND ( ( po.SchedDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN TRUNC(l.FrzStart, :"SYS_B_03") WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_04", :"SYS_B_05") THEN TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_06") WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_07", :"SYS_B_08") THEN TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_09") END ) + sp.MfgFrzDur/:"SYS_B_10", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE(:"SYS_B_11", :"SYS_B_12") then case when l.OHPost > TO_DATE(:"SYS_B_13", :"SYS_B_14") then TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_15") else TRUNC( s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_16") end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.MpsTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_17", TRUNC(sp.MPSTIMEFENCEDATE, :"SYS_B_18"))) AND po.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_19") OR ( po.SchedDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_20", :"SYS_B_21") THEN TRUNC(l.FrzStart, :"SYS_B_22") WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_25") WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_26", :"SYS_B_27") THEN TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_28") END ) + sp.MfgFrzDur/:"SYS_B_29", GREATEST(TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_30"), TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_31")) + sp.MfgLeadTime/:"SYS_B_32") )) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_33" )
_sql_text_map.7fyqfjhzfpy4h: SELECT /* CustOrderNonFcstTVQHint */ custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate, custorder.qty, custorder.reservation, custorder.resexp, custorder.supersedesw FROM PROCESSSKU, CUSTORDER WHERE ((custorder.fcstsw = :"SYS_B_0" and custorder.status != :"SYS_B_1")) AND ( CUSTORDER.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND CUSTORDER.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3 ) ) ORDER BY custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate ASC
_sql_text_map.7hfj1a7q6vd9h: DELETE /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANARRIV pa, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = pa.Item AND s.Loc = pa.Dest AND sp.Item = s.Item AND sp.Loc = s.loc AND ( ( pa.SchedArrivDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_03", :"SYS_B_04") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_05", :"SYS_B_06") THEN s.OHPost END)+ sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_07", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_08", :"SYS_B_09") then case when l.ohpost > TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_10", :"SYS_B_11") then l.ohpost else s.ohpost end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.DrpTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_12", sp.DrpTimeFenceDate)) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_13") OR ( pa.SchedArrivDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_14", :"SYS_B_15") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_16", :"SYS_B_17") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_18", :"SYS_B_19") THEN s.OHPost END) + sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_20", GREATEST((CASE WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_21", :"SYS_B_22") THEN s.OHPost WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN l.OHPost ELSE s.OHPost END)) + sp.PlanLeadTime/:"SYS_B_25")) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_26" ) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_27")
_sql_text_map.7n9pz49qw4f39: select decode(upper(failover_method), NULL, 0 , 'BASIC', 1, 'PRECONNECT', 2 , 'PREPARSE', 4 , 0), decode(upper(failover_type), NULL, 1, 'NONE', 1, 'SESSION', 2, 'SELECT', 4, 'TRANSACTION', 8, 1), failover_retries, failover_delay, flags, nvl(replay_initiation_timeout, 300), decode(upper(session_state_consistency), 'STATIC', 16, 0), failover_restore from sys.service$ where name = :1
_sql_text_map.85d154rq6wjur: SELECT /* CustOrderFcstTVQHint */ custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate, custorder.fcsttype, custorder.qty, custorder.reservation, custorder.resexp, custorder.supersedesw FROM PROCESSSKU, CUSTORDER WHERE ((custorder.fcstsw = :"SYS_B_0" and custorder.status != :"SYS_B_1")) AND ( CUSTORDER.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND CUSTORDER.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3 ) ) ORDER BY custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate ASC
_sql_text_map.865qwpcdyggkk: select spare6 from user$ where user#=:1
_sql_text_map.87fzyp4nvktqr: UPDATE SRE_NODE SET LAST_HEARTBEAT = SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) WHERE NODE_ID = :B2 AND ((LAST_HEARTBEAT IS NULL) OR ((SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) - LAST_HEARTBEAT) > :B1 ) OR ((LAST_HEARTBEAT - SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP)) > :B1 ) )
_sql_text_map.87r4zf1m45pkf: SELECT SSO_ID, SESSION_ID, CALLBACK_URL, USER_NAME, PRINCIPAL_ID, DIRTY_FLAG FROM SSO_MAP where SSO_ID = :1

SID, SAMPLE_TIME, TIME_INTERVAL, LENGTH_SEG, TM_LENGTH_SEG, END_SEG - TM_LENGTH_SEG TM_START_SEG, END_SEG - LENGTH_SEG START_SEG, END_SEG, TM_DELTA_CPU_TIME, TM_DELTA_DB_TIME, DELTA_READ_IO_REQUESTS, EXTRACT(SECOND FROM REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT) + EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT)*60 + EXTRACT(HOUR FROM REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT)*3600 + EXTRACT(DAY FROM REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT)*3600*24 END_SEG, sql_stats.sql_text: DELETE /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANARRIV pa, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = pa.Item AND s.Loc = pa.Dest AND sp.Item = s.Item AND sp.Loc = s.loc AND ( ( pa.SchedArrivDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_03", :"SYS_B_04") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_05", :"SYS_B_06") THEN s.OHPost END)+ sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_07", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_08", :"SYS_B_09") then case when l.ohpost > TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_10", :"SYS_B_11") then l.ohpost else s.ohpost end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.DrpTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_12", sp.DrpTimeFenceDate)) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_13") OR ( pa.SchedArrivDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_14", :"SYS_B_15") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_16", :"SYS_B_17") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_18", :"SYS_B_19") THEN s.OHPost END) + sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_20", GREATEST((CASE WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_21", :"SYS_B_22") THEN s.OHPost WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN l.OHPost ELSE s.OHPost END)) + sp.PlanLeadTime/:"SYS_B_25")) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_26" ) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_27")
sql_stats.sql_text_full: DELETE /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANARRIV pa, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = pa.Item AND s.Loc = pa.Dest AND sp.Item = s.Item AND sp.Loc = s.loc AND ( ( pa.SchedArrivDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_03", :"SYS_B_04") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_05", :"SYS_B_06") THEN s.OHPost END)+ sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_07", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_08", :"SYS_B_09") then case when l.ohpost > TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_10", :"SYS_B_11") then l.ohpost else s.ohpost end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.DrpTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_12", sp.DrpTimeFenceDate)) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_13") OR ( pa.SchedArrivDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_14", :"SYS_B_15") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_16", :"SYS_B_17") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_18", :"SYS_B_19") THEN s.OHPost END) + sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_20", GREATEST((CASE WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_21", :"SYS_B_22") THEN s.OHPost WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN l.OHPost ELSE s.OHPost END)) + sp.PlanLeadTime/:"SYS_B_25")) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_26" ) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_27")

sql_stats.sql_text: SELECT /*+ PARALLEL(32) */ /* CalculatePlanSelectHint */ SCPOMGR.ITEM.ITEM, SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC, SCPOMGR.ITEM.PLANLEVEL, SCPOMGR.SKU.PLANLEVEL, DECODE(SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.EXTERNALSKUSW, :"SYS_B_00", :"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02", :"SYS_B_03") , SCPOMGR.SKU.NETCHGSW as NETCHGSWORG , -:"SYS_B_04" FROM SCPOMGR.SKU , SCPOMGR.ITEM , SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM, SCPOMGR.LOC RELX_LOC where SCPOMGR.SKU.ITEM = SCPOMGR.ITEM.ITEM and SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.ITEM = SCPOMGR.SKU.ITEM and SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.LOC = SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC and (((RELX_LOC.LOC_TYPE = :"SYS_B_05" OR RELX_LOC.LOC_TYPE = :"SYS_B_06") AND (RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_07" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_08" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_09" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_10" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_11" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_12" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = : "SYS_B_13" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_14") AND (RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_15" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_16" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_17" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_18" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_19" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_20" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_21" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_22" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_23" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_24" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_25" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_26")) OR (RELX_LOC.LOC_TYPE = :"SYS_B_27" AND RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_28" AND (RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_29" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_30" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_31" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_32" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_33" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_34"))) and SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC=RELX_LOC.LOC ORDER B Y item.planlevel, item.item
sql_stats.sql_text_full: SELECT /*+ PARALLEL(32) */ /* CalculatePlanSelectHint */ SCPOMGR.ITEM.ITEM, SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC, SCPOMGR.ITEM.PLANLEVEL, SCPOMGR.SKU.PLANLEVEL, DECODE(SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.EXTERNALSKUSW, :"SYS_B_00", :"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02", :"SYS_B_03") , SCPOMGR.SKU.NETCHGSW as NETCHGSWORG , -:"SYS_B_04" FROM SCPOMGR.SKU , SCPOMGR.ITEM , SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM, SCPOMGR.LOC RELX_LOC where SCPOMGR.SKU.ITEM = SCPOMGR.ITEM.ITEM and SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.ITEM = SCPOMGR.SKU.ITEM and SCPOMGR.SKUPLANNINGPARAM.LOC = SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC and (((RELX_LOC.LOC_TYPE = :"SYS_B_05" OR RELX_LOC.LOC_TYPE = :"SYS_B_06") AND (RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_07" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_08" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_09" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_10" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_11" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_12" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = : "SYS_B_13" OR RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_14") AND (RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_15" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_16" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_17" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_18" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_19" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_20" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_21" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_22" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_23" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_24" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_25" OR RELX_LOC.U_ROG = :"SYS_B_26")) OR (RELX_LOC.LOC_TYPE = :"SYS_B_27" AND RELX_LOC.U_DIVISION = :"SYS_B_28" AND (RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_29" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_30" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_31" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_32" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_33" OR RELX_LOC.U_DST_CNTR = :"SYS_B_34"))) and SCPOMGR.SKU.LOC=RELX_LOC.LOC ORDER B Y item.planlevel, item.item

sql_stats.sql_text: DELETE /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANORDER po, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = po.Item AND s.Loc = po.Loc AND sp.loc = s.loc AND sp.item = s.item AND ( ( po.SchedDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN TRUNC(l.FrzStart, :"SYS_B_03") WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_04", :"SYS_B_05") THEN TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_06") WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_07", :"SYS_B_08") THEN TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_09") END ) + sp.MfgFrzDur/:"SYS_B_10", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE(:"SYS_B_11", :"SYS_B_12") then case when l.OHPost > TO_DATE(:"SYS_B_13", :"SYS_B_14") then TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_15") else TRUNC( s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_16") end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.MpsTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_17", TRUNC(sp.MPSTIMEFENCEDATE, :"SYS_B_18"))) AND po.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_19") OR ( po.SchedDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_20", :"SYS_B_21") THEN TRUNC(l.FrzStart, :"SYS_B_22") WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_25") WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_26", :"SYS_B_27") THEN TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_28") END ) + sp.MfgFrzDur/:"SYS_B_29", GREATEST(TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_30"), TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_31")) + sp.MfgLeadTime/:"SYS_B_32") )) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_33" )
sql_stats.sql_text_full: DELETE /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANORDER po, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = po.Item AND s.Loc = po.Loc AND sp.loc = s.loc AND sp.item = s.item AND ( ( po.SchedDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN TRUNC(l.FrzStart, :"SYS_B_03") WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_04", :"SYS_B_05") THEN TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_06") WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_07", :"SYS_B_08") THEN TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_09") END ) + sp.MfgFrzDur/:"SYS_B_10", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE(:"SYS_B_11", :"SYS_B_12") then case when l.OHPost > TO_DATE(:"SYS_B_13", :"SYS_B_14") then TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_15") else TRUNC( s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_16") end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.MpsTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_17", TRUNC(sp.MPSTIMEFENCEDATE, :"SYS_B_18"))) AND po.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_19") OR ( po.SchedDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_20", :"SYS_B_21") THEN TRUNC(l.FrzStart, :"SYS_B_22") WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_25") WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_26", :"SYS_B_27") THEN TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_28") END ) + sp.MfgFrzDur/:"SYS_B_29", GREATEST(TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_30"), TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_31")) + sp.MfgLeadTime/:"SYS_B_32") )) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_33" )

6sbg5ynywhk0z.fullText: DELETE /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANORDER po, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = po.Item AND s.Loc = po.Loc AND sp.loc = s.loc AND sp.item = s.item AND ( ( po.SchedDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN TRUNC(l.FrzStart, :"SYS_B_03") WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_04", :"SYS_B_05") THEN TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_06") WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_07", :"SYS_B_08") THEN TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_09") END ) + sp.MfgFrzDur/:"SYS_B_10", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE(:"SYS_B_11", :"SYS_B_12") then case when l.OHPost > TO_DATE(:"SYS_B_13", :"SYS_B_14") then TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_15") else TRUNC( s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_16") end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.MpsTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_17", TRUNC(sp.MPSTIMEFENCEDATE, :"SYS_B_18"))) AND po.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_19") OR ( po.SchedDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_20", :"SYS_B_21") THEN TRUNC(l.FrzStart, :"SYS_B_22") WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_25") WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_26", :"SYS_B_27") THEN TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_28") END ) + sp.MfgFrzDur/:"SYS_B_29", GREATEST(TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_30"), TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_31")) + sp.MfgLeadTime/:"SYS_B_32") )) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_33" )

7fyqfjhzfpy4h.fullText: SELECT /* CustOrderNonFcstTVQHint */ custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate, custorder.qty, custorder.reservation, custorder.resexp, custorder.supersedesw FROM PROCESSSKU, CUSTORDER WHERE ((custorder.fcstsw = :"SYS_B_0" and custorder.status != :"SYS_B_1")) AND ( CUSTORDER.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND CUSTORDER.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3 ) ) ORDER BY custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate ASC

7hfj1a7q6vd9h.fullText: DELETE /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANARRIV pa, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = pa.Item AND s.Loc = pa.Dest AND sp.Item = s.Item AND sp.Loc = s.loc AND ( ( pa.SchedArrivDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_03", :"SYS_B_04") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_05", :"SYS_B_06") THEN s.OHPost END)+ sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_07", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_08", :"SYS_B_09") then case when l.ohpost > TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_10", :"SYS_B_11") then l.ohpost else s.ohpost end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.DrpTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_12", sp.DrpTimeFenceDate)) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_13") OR ( pa.SchedArrivDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_14", :"SYS_B_15") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_16", :"SYS_B_17") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_18", :"SYS_B_19") THEN s.OHPost END) + sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_20", GREATEST((CASE WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_21", :"SYS_B_22") THEN s.OHPost WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN l.OHPost ELSE s.OHPost END)) + sp.PlanLeadTime/:"SYS_B_25")) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_26" ) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_27")

BUCKET_START_OFFSET) / LENGTH_SEG ELSE 1 END FIRST_BUCKET_RATIO, CASE WHEN BUCKET_END >= BUCKET_START + 2 THEN :B7 / LENGTH_SEG ELSE NULL END MIDDLE_BUCKETS_RATIO, CASE WHEN BUCKET_START != BUCKET_END WHEN BUCKET_END >= TM_BUCKET_START + 2 THEN :B7 / TM_LENGTH_SEG ELSE NULL END TM_MIDDLE_BUCKETS_RATIO, CASE WHEN TM_BUCKET_START != BUCKET_END THEN BUCKET_END_ OFFSET / TM_LENGTH_SEG ELSE NULL END TM_LAST_BUCKET_RATIO, TM_DELTA_CPU_TIME, TM_DELTA_DB_TIME, DELTA_READ_IO_REQUESTS DELTA_READ_REQUESTS, DELTA_WRITE_IO_REQUESTS DELTA_WRITE_REQUESTS, DELTA_READ_IO_BYTES DELTA_READ_BYTES, TM_BUCKET_START_OFFSET, TRUNC(END_SEG/:B7 ) BUCKET_END, MOD(END_SEG, :B7 ) BUCKET_END_OFFSET, END_SEG, LENGTH_SEG, TM_LENGTH_SEG, TM_DELTA_CPU_TIME, TM_DELTA_DB_TIME, DELTA_READ_IO_REQUESTS, TIME_INTERVAL, CASE WHEN START_SEG < 0 THEN 0 ELSE START_SEG END START_SEG, CASE WHEN TM_START_SEG < 0 THEN 0 ELSE TM_START_SEG END TM_START_SEG, CASE WHEN END_SEG > TIME_INTERVAL THEN TIME_INTERVAL ELSE END_SEG END END_SEG, (LENGTH_SEG - (CASE WHEN START_SEG < 0 THEN -START_SEG ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN END_SEG > TIME_INTERVAL THEN END_SEG - TIME_INTERVAL ELSE 0 END)) LENGTH_SEG, (TM_LENGTH_SEG - (CASE WHEN TM_START_SEG < 0 THEN -TM_START_SEG ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN END_SEG > TIME_INTERVAL THEN END_SEG - TIME_INTERVAL ELSE 0 END)) TM_LENGTH_SEG, TM_DELTA_CPU_TIME, TM_DELTA_DB_TIME, DELTA_READ_IO_REQUESTS, DELTA_WRITE_IO_REQUESTS, DELTA_READ_IO_BYTES, DELTA_WRITE_IO_BYTES, DELTA_INTERCONNECT_IO_BYTES, DELTA_READ_MEM_BYTES, PGA_ALLOCATED, TEMP_SPACE_ALLOCATED FROM (SELECT ROW_ID, SID, SAMPLE_TIME, TIME_INTERVAL, LENGTH_SEG, TM_LENGTH_SEG, END_SEG - TM_LENGTH_SEG TM_START_SEG, END_SEG - LENGTH_SEG START_SEG, END_SEG, TM_DELTA_CPU_TIME, TM_DELTA_DB_TIME, DELTA_READ_IO_REQUESTS, DELTA_WRITE_IO_REQUESTS, DELTA_READ_IO_BYTES, DELTA_WRITE_IO_BYTES, DELTA_INTERCONNECT_IO_BYTES, DELTA_READ_MEM_BYTES, PGA_ALLOCATED, TEMP_SPACE_ALLOCATED FROM (SELECT ROW_ID, SID, SAMPLE_TIME, EXTRACT(SECOND FROM REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT) + EXTRACT(MINUTE FROM REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT)*60 + EXTRACT(HOUR FROM REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT)*3600 + EXTRACT(DAY FROM REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT)*3600*24 END_SEG, TIME_INTERVAL, DELTA_TIME/1000000 LENGTH_SEG, TM_DELTA_TIME/1000000 TM_LENGTH_SEG, TM_DELTA_CPU_TIME, TM_DELTA_DB_TIME, DELTA_READ_IO_REQUESTS, DELTA_WRITE_IO_REQUESTS, DELTA_READ_IO_BYTES, DELTA_WRITE_IO_BYTES, DELTA_INTERCONNECT_IO_BYTES, DELTA_READ_MEM_BYTES, PGA_ALLOCATED, TEMP_SPACE_ALLOCATED FROM (SELECT ROWNUM ROW_ID, SESSION_ID SID, CAST(FROM_TZ(SAMPLE_TIME, DBTIMEZONE) AS DATE) SAMPLE_DATE, SAMPLE_TIME, (SAMPLE_TIME - CAST(:B6 AS TIMESTAMP)) REL_SAMPLE_TIME_INT, TRUNC((:B5 - :B6 ) * 3600 * 24) TIME_INTERVAL, :B6 AS BND_START_INTERVAL , :B5 AS BND_END_INTERVAL , TM_DELTA_TIME, TM_DELTA_CPU_TIME, TM_DELTA_DB_TIME, DELTA_TIME, DELTA_READ_IO_REQUESTS, DELTA_WRITE_IO_REQUESTS, DELTA_READ_IO_BYTES, DELTA_WRITE_IO_BYTES, DELTA_INTERCONNECT_IO_BYTES, DELTA_READ_MEM_BYTES, PGA_ALLOCATED, TEMP_SPACE_ALLOCATED FROM (SELECT ASH0.* FROM V$ACTIVE_SESSION_HISTORY ASH0 WHERE ((SESSION_ID = :B2 AND SESSION_SERIAL# = :B1 AND USERENV('INSTANCE') = :B3 ) OR (:B4 = 'yes' AND QC_INSTANCE_ID = :B3 AND QC_SESSION_ID = :B2 AND QC_SESSION_SERIAL# = :B1 ))) ) ASH1 WHERE SAMPLE_DATE >= BND_START_INTERVAL AND SAMPLE_DATE <= BND_END_INTERVAL + 120/3600/24 ) ) WHERE START_SEG <= TIME_INTERVAL ) WHERE (LENGTH_SEG IS NOT NULL AND LENGTH_SEG > 0) OR (TM_LENGTH_SEG IS NOT NULL AND TM_LENGTH_SEG > 0)) ) GROUP BY INST_ID, GROUP_TYPE, BUCKET_START, BUCKET_END, TM_GROUP_TYPE, TM_BUCKET_START, TM_BUCKET_END))) GVTF WHERE INST_ID >= :B9 AND INST_ID <= :B8 GROUP BY GROUP_TYPE, BUCKET_START , BUCKET_END, TM_GROUP_TYPE, TM_BUCKET_START, TM_BUCKET_END ) GROUP BY GROUPING SETS ( (GROUP_TYPE, BUCKET_START, BUCKET_END), (TM_GROUP_TYPE, TM_BUCKET_START, TM_BUCKET_END) )

8whdr1ajd43dk.fullText: SELECT /* IntransInTVQHint */ vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate, vehicleload.deststatus, vehicleload.sourcestatus, vehicleloadline.actionallowedsw, vehicleloadline.expdate, vehicleloadline.primaryitem, vehicleloadline.qty, vehicleloadline.reviseddate, vehicleloadline.revisedexpdate FROM PROCESSSKU, VEHICLELOAD, VEHICLELOADLINE WHERE ( VEHICLELOAD.LOADID=VEHICLELOADLINE.LOADID) AND ( VEHICLELOADLINE.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND VEHICLELOADLINE.DEST=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3 ) ) ORDER BY vehicleloadline.item, vehicleloadline.dest, vehicleload.arrivdate, vehicleloadline.schedarrivdate ASC

SCPOMGR.SLCSTATS.MODEL=SCPOMGR.DFU.MODEL and SCPOMGR.FCSTPERFSTATIC.LAG = :"SYS_B_0" and SCPOMGR.FCSTPERFSTATIC.FCSTDATE = to_date(:"SYS_B_1", :"SYS_B_2") and SCPOMGR.SLCSTATS.D _sql_text_map.6sbg5ynywhk0z: DELETE /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANORDER po, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = po.Item AND s.Loc = po.Loc AND sp.loc = s.loc AND sp.item = s.item AND ( ( po.SchedDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN TRUNC(l.FrzStart, :"SYS_B_03") WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_04", :"SYS_B_05") THEN TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_06") WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_07", :"SYS_B_08") THEN TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_09") END ) + sp.MfgFrzDur/:"SYS_B_10", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE(:"SYS_B_11", :"SYS_B_12") then case when l.OHPost > TO_DATE(:"SYS_B_13", :"SYS_B_14") then TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_15") else TRUNC( s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_16") end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.MpsTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_17", TRUNC(sp.MPSTIMEFENCEDATE, :"SYS_B_18"))) AND po.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_19") OR ( po.SchedDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_20", :"SYS_B_21") THEN TRUNC(l.FrzStart, :"SYS_B_22") WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_25") WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_26", :"SYS_B_27") THEN TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_28") END ) + sp.MfgFrzDur/:"SYS_B_29", GREATEST(TRUNC(s.OHPost, :"SYS_B_30"), TRUNC(l.OHPost, :"SYS_B_31")) + sp.MfgLeadTime/:"SYS_B_32") )) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanOrderBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_33" )
_sql_text_map.7fyqfjhzfpy4h: SELECT /* CustOrderNonFcstTVQHint */ custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate, custorder.qty, custorder.reservation, custorder.resexp, custorder.supersedesw FROM PROCESSSKU, CUSTORDER WHERE ((custorder.fcstsw = :"SYS_B_0" and custorder.status != :"SYS_B_1")) AND ( CUSTORDER.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND CUSTORDER.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3 ) ) ORDER BY custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate ASC
_sql_text_map.7hfj1a7q6vd9h: DELETE /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeDeleteHint */ FROM ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeInlineViewHint */ s.Item Item, l.Loc Loc FROM SKU s, SKUPlanningParam sp, PLANARRIV pa, LOC l WHERE s.Loc = l.Loc AND s.ReplenType = :"SYS_B_00" AND s.Item = pa.Item AND s.Loc = pa.Dest AND sp.Item = s.Item AND sp.Loc = s.loc AND ( ( pa.SchedArrivDate >= GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_01", :"SYS_B_02") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_03", :"SYS_B_04") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_05", :"SYS_B_06") THEN s.OHPost END)+ sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_07", GREATEST( case when sp.LASTPLANSTART <= TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_08", :"SYS_B_09") then case when l.ohpost > TO_DATE (:"SYS_B_10", :"SYS_B_11") then l.ohpost else s.ohpost end else sp.LASTPLANSTART end + sp.DrpTimeFenceDur/:"SYS_B_12", sp.DrpTimeFenceDate)) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_13") OR ( pa.SchedArrivDate < GREATEST((CASE WHEN l.FrzStart > to_date(:"SYS_B_14", :"SYS_B_15") THEN l.FrzStart WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_16", :"SYS_B_17") THEN l.OHPost WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_18", :"SYS_B_19") THEN s.OHPost END) + sp.DrpFrzDur/:"SYS_B_20", GREATEST((CASE WHEN s.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_21", :"SYS_B_22") THEN s.OHPost WHEN l.OHPost > to_date(:"SYS_B_23", :"SYS_B_24") THEN l.OHPost ELSE s.OHPost END)) + sp.PlanLeadTime/:"SYS_B_25")) AND pa.FirmPlanSw = :"SYS_B_26" ) ) WHERE (Item, Loc) IN ( SELECT /* PlanArrivalBatchPurgeBatchSelectHint */ Item, Loc FROM ProcessSku ps WHERE ProcessID = :1 AND ItemLevel = :2 AND BatchNum = :3 AND NetChgSw = :"SYS_B_27")
_sql_text_map.7n9pz49qw4f39: select decode(upper(failover_method), NULL, 0 , 'BASIC', 1, 'PRECONNECT', 2 , 'PREPARSE', 4 , 0), decode(upper(failover_type), NULL, 1, 'NONE', 1, 'SESSION', 2, 'SELECT', 4, 'TRANSACTION', 8, 1), failover_retries, failover_delay, flags, nvl(replay_initiation_timeout, 300), decode(upper(session_state_consistency), 'STATIC', 16, 0), failover_restore from sys.service$ where name = :1
_sql_text_map.85d154rq6wjur: SELECT /* CustOrderFcstTVQHint */ custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate, custorder.fcsttype, custorder.qty, custorder.reservation, custorder.resexp, custorder.supersedesw FROM PROCESSSKU, CUSTORDER WHERE ((custorder.fcstsw = :"SYS_B_0" and custorder.status != :"SYS_B_1")) AND ( CUSTORDER.ITEM=PROCESSSKU.ITEM AND CUSTORDER.LOC=PROCESSSKU.LOC) AND ( ( processsku.processid = :1 and processsku.itemlevel = :2 and processsku.batchnum = :3 ) ) ORDER BY custorder.item, custorder.loc, custorder.shipdate ASC
_sql_text_map.87fzyp4nvktqr: UPDATE SRE_NODE SET LAST_HEARTBEAT = SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) WHERE NODE_ID = :B2 AND ((LAST_HEARTBEAT IS NULL) OR ((SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) - LAST_HEARTBEAT) > :B1 ) OR ((LAST_HEARTBEAT - SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP)) > :B1 ) )

TIME_INTERVAL, CASE WHEN START_SEG < 0 THEN 0 ELSE START_SEG END START_SEG, CASE WHEN TM_START_SEG < 0 THEN 0 ELSE TM_START_SEG END TM_START_SEG, CASE WHEN END_SEG > TIME_INTERVAL THEN TIME_INTERVAL ELSE END_SEG END END_SEG, (LENGTH_SEG - (CASE WHEN START_SEG < 0 THEN -START_SEG ELSE 0 END + CASE WHEN END_SEG > TIME_INTERVAL THEN END_SEG - TIME_INTERVAL ELSE 0 END)) LENGTH_SEG, (TM_LENGTH_SEG - _sql_text_map.anvdfxu0vqu6j: MERGE INTO U_DFUEXTFCST_AUDIT A USING (SELECT /*+ parallel(32) */ DMDUNIT, DMDGROUP, LOC, STARTDATE, DUR, FCST_QTY, NVL(FCST_QTY_LY, 0) AS FCST_QTY_LY, SHIP_HIST_QTY_LY, SHIP_HIST_QTY_2Y, SALES_HIST_QTY_LY, SALES_HIST_QTY_2Y, AGG_PI_QTY, MEDIAN_PS_QTY, FCSTID, MODEL, :B1 AS AUDIT_DATE FROM U_DFUEXTFCST ) DE ON (A.DMDUNIT = DE.DMDUNIT AND A.DMDGROUP = DE.DMDGROUP AND A.LOC = DE.LOC AND A.STARTDATE = DE.STARTDATE AND A.DUR = DE.DUR AND A.AUDIT_DATE = DE.AUDIT_DATE) WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (A.DMDUNIT, A.DMDGROUP, A.LOC, A.STARTDATE, A.DUR, A.FCST_QTY, A.FCST_QTY_LY, A.SHIP_HIST_QTY_LY, A.SHIP_HIST_QTY_2Y, A.SALES_HIST_QTY_LY, A.SALES_HIST_QTY_2Y, A.AGG_PI_QTY, A.MEDIAN_PS_QTY, A.FCSTID, A.MODEL, A.AUDIT_DATE) VALUES (DE.DMDUNIT, DE.DMDGROUP, DE.LOC, DE.STARTDATE, DE.DUR, DE.FCST_QTY, DE.FCST_QTY_LY, DE.SHIP_HIST_QTY_LY, DE.SHIP_HIST_QTY_2Y, DE.SALES_HIST_QTY_LY, DE.SALES_HIST_QTY_2Y, DE.AGG_PI_QTY, DE.MEDIAN_PS_QTY, DE.FCSTID, DE.MODEL, D E.AUDIT_DATE)
_sql_text_map.aq0t5ju2h9tjh: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM SRE_JOB, SRE_NODE_POOL_CONFIG WHERE SRE_JOB.SERVICE_NAME = SRE_NODE_POOL_CONFIG.SERVICE_NAME AND SRE_JOB.NODE_CONFIG_NAME = SRE_NODE_POOL_CONFIG.NODE_CONFIG_NAME AND SRE_NODE_POOL_CONFIG.POOL_NAME = :B4 AND ((JOB_TYPE = :B3 AND IS_CONTINUOUS_ENABLED = 1) OR (JOB_TYPE = :B2 ) ) AND JOB_STATE = :B1 AND SRE_JOB.JOB_ID IN (SELECT JOB_ID FROM (SELECT JOB_ID, ROWNUM JOBROWNUM FROM (SELECT JOB_ID FROM SRE_JOB WHERE ((((JOB_TYPE = :B3 AND IS_CONTINUOUS_ENABLED = 1) OR (JOB_TYPE = :B2 ) ) AND JOB_STATE = :B1 ) OR (JOB_ID = :B5 ) ) AND LOCKERS_NODE_ID IS NULL AND SUBMISSION_DATE <= SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) ORDER BY JOB_TYPE DESC, JOB_PRIORITY DESC, SUBMISSION_DATE, JOB_ID ) ) WHERE JOBROWNUM < (SELECT MIN(CANDIDATEROWNUM) + :B6 FROM (SELECT JOB_ID, ROWNUM CANDIDATEROWNUM FROM (SELECT JOB_ID FROM SRE_JOB WHERE ((((JOB_TYPE = :B3 AND IS_CONTINUOUS_ENABLED = 1) OR (JOB_TYPE = :B2 ) ) AND JOB_STATE = :B1 ) OR (JOB_ID = :B5 ) ) AND LOCKERS_NODE_ID IS NULL AND SUB MISSION_DATE <= SYS_EXTRACT_UTC(SYSTIMESTAMP) ORDER BY JOB_TYPE DESC, JOB_PRIORITY DESC, SUBMISSION_DATE, JOB_ID ) ) WHERE JOB_ID = :B5 ) )
